title: K and K+Si medium
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"doo6a","text":"Medium to grow most phytoplankton species in small eukaryotes (Mamiellophyceae, Pelagophyceae etc...).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bpcr1","text":"When using for diatoms and coccolithophorids add silica (Si).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"776r8","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"9oftr","text":"Reference","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"3l5ph","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bnfr3","text":"Keller, M.D., Selvin, R.C., Claus, W. \u0026 Guillard, R.R.L. 1987. Media for the culture of oceanic ultraphytoplankton. J. Phycol. 23:633–8.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"51m0f","text":"Please refer to our general recommendations to grow cultures :","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b8bd9","text":"\nhttps://www.protocols.io/private/A48906DC1374AD6281495CB86A8F092F","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Filter 1L of old seawater of at least two months on prefilter and filter 0,2 microns
Under hood, to seawater, add these nutriments that have been autoclaved (excepted vitamin) : %s %s
K Trace Metal Solution To 900 mL of H 2 O add : %s %s Make final volume up to 1.0L using H2O. Heat to dissolve

f/2 Vitamin solution In 100mL of distilled water, dissolve 0,05g of biotin (vit. H) and 0,05g of cyanocobalamin (vit. B 12 ) = solution A In 0,5mL of solution A, add 0,05g of thiamine HCl (vit. B 1 ) and complete final volume to 50mL of distilled water Filter sterilize on Millipore 0,22µm Store in refrigerator or freezer
creator: Daniel Vaulot
